result.item: AUDIT ITEM 4 — "In raw-A, checker (evaluator gate) AND entailment (NLI faithfulness) COMPLETELY off." Tree /home/polaris/wt/phase-file; trace compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py -> generate_multi_section_report.
result.status: GAP-FOUND — the audit claim is HALF WRONG. CHECKER/EXTERNAL-EVALUATOR: OFF (confirmed). ENTAILMENT/NLI: ON in enforce mode (NOT off — claim refuted). So "COMPLETELY off" is false for entailment.
result.evidence:
CHECKER GATE = OFF (confirmed):
- scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py imports only: multi_section_generator (line 271), openrouter_client (274), outline_agent (277), data_loader (295), planning_gate_schema (314), compose_render_projection (317), contract_compliance (485). NO evaluator/external_evaluator import; grep for run_external_evaluation/ExternalEvaluator in the script = zero.
- generate_multi_section_report docstring, multi_section_generator.py:9787-9789 (quoted): "This function does NOT call the evaluator -- run_external_evaluation is invoked by the orchestrator." Grep of the whole generator for `run_external_evaluation(` call sites = zero (only PT11-mirror comments; the sole real import from external_evaluator is `_next_real_sentence_end` for sentence-splitting at line 8067, not the gate).
- The 4 real callers of run_external_evaluation are the honest-sweep orchestrators: provenance_verified_pipeline.py:438, run_honest_on_prerebuild_corpus.py:418, run_honest_sweep_r3.py:18573, run_live_honest_cycle.py:361 — NONE is this compose driver.
- The script's only post-compose audits are _audit_citations() (regex [CITE:ev_] leak tripwire, script:466) and an OPTIONAL disclosure-only contract-compliance audit (script:474, gated on --gate-artifact). Neither is the evaluator/judge gate.

ENTAILMENT/NLI = ON, enforce (claim refuted):
- generate_multi_section_report uses provenance_generator.strict_verify (imported multi_section_generator.py:56; executed per section at :6399). strict_verify runs the per-sentence entailment judge: provenance_generator.py:2568 `mode = _entailment_mode()`, guarded `if mode in ("warn","enforce")` (:2569), calling `_get_judge().judge(sentence_clean, combined_span)` at :2587-2589. Clinical sibling has the parallel live gate at clinical_generator/strict_verify.py:527-529.
- _entailment_mode() (clinical_generator/strict_verify.py:358-379) reads PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT with _DEFAULT_MODE = "enforce" (line 355; I-bug-095 flipped default off->enforce). The strict_verify.py:404 docstring "off by default" is STALE — code default is enforce.
- The compose script sets PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT ZERO times (grep -c = 0) -> resolves to enforce -> the judge FIRES per verified sentence.
- _get_judge() returns a real live LLM judge (_EntailmentJudge, entailment_judge.py:632; default model z-ai/glm-5.2 at :107), fail-CLOSED on judge_error in enforce (drops the sentence).
result.codex_verdict: GATES-PARTIAL — codex-sol independently traced both files line-by-line and emitted: "Checker/external-evaluator: OFF (compose driver imports no evaluator; generator has zero run_external_evaluation calls; the 4 real callers are the honest-sweep orchestrators). Entailment/NLI: ON, enforce (_DEFAULT_MODE='enforce' at strict_verify.py:345/358; live _EntailmentJudge z-ai/glm-5.2; neither driver nor .env sets PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT; the 'off by default' text at line 404 is stale)." Codex added one refinement: an inherited explicit PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off env var remains a documented escape hatch, and the script has a second (optional, disclosure-only) contract-compliance audit besides _audit_citations.
result.gaps: The audit's twin assertion is only 50% correct. CHECKER: correctly OFF on raw-A (no external evaluator/PT-rule gate runs in this compose path; only a regex citation tripwire + optional disclosure audit). ENTAILMENT/NLI: the audit's "completely off" is FALSE — strict_verify's entailment judge runs in enforce mode by default (PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT unset -> enforce), fail-closed, against a live glm-5.2 judge. The only way entailment would be off on raw-A is an EXTERNALLY-INHERITED PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT=off in the shell env (the script itself never sets it, and .env has no override) — not verifiable from source alone but not the default. NOTE: the deterministic strict_verify layers (provenance-token/span-bounds/decimal/percent/qualifier/overlap checks, provenance_generator + strict_verify.py:423-521) run REGARDLESS of entailment mode, so even a hypothetical entailment=off still leaves substantial faithfulness gating live.
result.recommendation: Correct the audit line to read: "raw-A compose (compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py) runs WITHOUT the external evaluator/checker gate (that lives only in the honest-sweep orchestrators), but WITH strict_verify's entailment/NLI in enforce mode (default) plus the full deterministic provenance/span/numeric gates." If the initiative genuinely needs the external evaluator on this path, wire run_external_evaluation into the driver after report.md assembly (mirror scripts/run_honest_on_prerebuild_corpus.py:418) — scope: add the evaluator import + one post-compose call + a return-code gate; ~30-50 lines, no generator change. To make entailment status auditable rather than inferred, have the driver os.environ.setdefault('PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT','enforce') and log the effective mode. Do NOT implement now — investigate-then-consult: confirm with the operator whether raw-A is SUPPOSED to skip the evaluator (by design it is the composition-only driver, evaluation delegated to the orchestrator) before adding a gate.

result.item: Deliverable 5 — single clean reproducible run command: scripts/run_raw_a.sh
result.evidence: Created /home/polaris/wt/phase-file/scripts/run_raw_a.sh (executable). `bash -n scripts/run_raw_a.sh` => exit 0 (BASH_N_OK); `--help` renders the header. It wraps the confirmed-safe driver scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py (its argparse at lines 172-191 defines --corpus/--out-dir/--rq-drb-task default "72", and it hard-requires OPENROUTER_API_KEY in env). All 8 knobs present and verified: (1) LD_LIBRARY_PATH via `cat` of the pinned browserlibs/LDPATH.txt (read from file, not hardcoded); (2) PG_LOOPBACK_MODE=0; (3) PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 + PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_SCORE_CHUNK=16 + PYTORCH_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True; (4) PG_OUTLINE_MAX_TOKENS=131072 + PG_OUTLINE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS=32768; (5) OPENROUTER_API_KEY/SERPER_API_KEY via /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python dotenv_values() — I REPRODUCED the memo's line-304 breakage: `. ./.env` on /workspace/POLARIS/.env errors `line 304: paper: command not found` because line 304 is `PG_EXA_CATEGORY=research paper` (unquoted space), while dotenv_values reads it as 'research paper' and both keys extract True; (6) `unset PYTHONPATH`; (7) interpreter /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python (python3.11); (8) --corpus default data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json (the proven corpus per scripts/_run_16way_s3gear329.sh), --rq-drb-task 72, --out-dir outputs/raw_a_run, resolved against script-derived REPO_ROOT for reproducibility. Header comment documents each knob + WHY. No paid compose was run. Codex prompt written to /tmp/codex_runscript.md.
result.codex_verdict: RUNSCRIPT-SOUND — "All eight required knobs are present with the specified values and behavior. API keys use dotenv_values, PYTHONPATH is unset, paths and defaults are sane, documentation explains each knob and rationale, and bash -n scripts/run_raw_a.sh passes. No missing or incorrect required settings found." (Note: first gate run emitted a spurious RUNSCRIPT-REVISE that HALTED BEFORE reading the script on out-of-scope repo-governance preconditions — missing state/beatboth_campaign/loop_state.json and canonical-pin doc drift — unrelated to the script; re-running with those preconditions explicitly scoped out produced the RUNSCRIPT-SOUND verdict after a genuine line-by-line read.)
result.gaps: none — script created, syntax-valid, all 8 knobs correct, Codex line-by-line SOUND. One environmental caveat (not a script defect): this phase-file worktree is a file-rename tree and does not itself carry data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json or .env; the script's defaults are repo-root-relative (corpus) and point at /workspace/POLARIS/.env (overridable via PG_ENV_FILE), which is where the canonical run resolves them — the proven corpus copy exists at /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json. The script fails loud with distinct exit codes (3-6) if any pinned path is absent.
result.recommendation: To make a real invocation fully self-contained regardless of which worktree it runs in, either (a) run it from a worktree that carries data/ and .env, or (b) pass --corpus explicitly and set PG_ENV_FILE to the intended .env. No code change required for the deliverable as specified. Executing an actual paid compose to end-to-end validate the run was intentionally NOT done per instructions.
